theme: Make the silent things speak — conference table, whiteboard, and ficus deserve runtime stories
visual_observations: Review performed via code analysis (container timed out during Puppeteer). Office floor has conference table always empty, ficus growth stages defined but streak counter invisible to users, whiteboard shows vibe text but no task count or worker names, aisle markers are static decorations, ambient particles identical across all vibes despite CSS hooks existing.

ideas.title: Conference Table Should Host Standup When 3+ Workers Active
ideas.description: The conference-table element in Office.jsx renders an empty SVG ring with a conf-meeting-badge that only appears via data-meeting attribute — but nothing ever sets it. When activeWorkers.length >= 3 and the vibe is 'crushing' or 'in-flow', set data-meeting on the conference table and render tiny seated Character avatars (.char--seated, already styled at 16px) around the ring. This transforms dead real estate into a visual narrative: 'the team is so in sync they're huddling.' The CSS already exists (.conf-meeting-badge, .char--seated) — it's just never triggered.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Should Show Active Task Count Not Just Vibe
ideas.description: The .office-whiteboard currently renders wb-vibe-text (the vibe label) and a progress bar. But it never shows HOW MANY tasks are in flight. Add a wb-task-count line above the progress bar: '3 tasks · 2 active' in 0.4rem text. The whiteboard is the team's shared information radiator — in a real office it would show sprint progress. The data is already computed in App.jsx (workers array with status field). Pass taskSummary={active: N, total: N} as a prop to Office, then to the Whiteboard sub-component.

ideas.title: Ficus Streak Counter — Show the Growth Number
ideas.description: The office-plant--ficus has 5 growth stages (seedling→blooming) driven by vibeStreak, with milestone burst animations already coded. But users have no idea what the number IS or what triggers growth. Add a tiny tooltip on hover showing 'Streak: 4 🌱' (or whatever stage name). The .office-plant already has pointer-events: auto and cursor: default — it's waiting for interaction. Users who discover the ficus growth mechanic will check it obsessively, creating a retention loop around vibe streaks.

ideas.title: Ambient Particles Should Match Vibe Color — CSS Hooks Exist But Are Disconnected
ideas.description: App.css defines vibe-specific particle colors: [data-vibe='crushing'] .ambient-particle { background: #4ade80 }, [data-vibe='on-fire'] { background: #f87171 }, etc. But Office.css also defines .ambient-particle with background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12) which likely overrides due to specificity. Check that the data-vibe attribute on the ambient-particles container (or a parent) propagates correctly. If particles are always white, the vibe-reactive colors are dead code. This is a bug-fix disguised as polish — the work was already done, it just doesn't render.

ideas.title: Door Should Show Recent Arrivals/Departures as Mini-Log
ideas.description: The office-door has arrive/depart swing animations and a notify-dot, but no persistent indication of WHO just arrived or left. Add a small .door-recent-log absolutely positioned beside the door showing the last 1-2 events: '🌙 Luna arrived 3m ago'. The activity feed has this data (hire/complete events with timestamps). This turns the door from a passive decoration into a real-time narrative element — like the lobby board in a coworking space.

ideas.title: Desk Hover Should Show Worker's Current File (From Task String)
ideas.description: Worker tasks often contain filenames like 'Fix responsive layout in Office.css'. The char-hover-card shows name, role, task, and time — but could parse and highlight the filename from the task string with a mono-font code badge. Regex: /\b[\w-]+\.(jsx?|tsx?|css|json|md)\b/. This makes the hover card feel like peeking over someone's shoulder at their screen — a delightful detail that costs one regex.

ideas.title: Keyboard Shortcut '?' Should Open a Help Overlay
ideas.description: The shortcut-toast component exists and shows keyboard hints, but there's no comprehensive help panel. Pressing '?' should toggle a modal listing all shortcuts (1-5 for worker focus, T for tab toggle, etc.) and a mini legend of vibe states. Many dashboards have this (GitHub, Gmail). The toast infrastructure is already there — extend it to a full overlay. This lowers the learning curve for first-time visitors.

ideas.title: End-of-Day Timelapse — Compress 24h of Office Life into 30s
ideas.description: Store the last 24h of vibe snapshots (already in sparkline data) and worker state changes. Add a 'Replay' button in the dashboard that animates through the day: sky changes, workers arriving/leaving, vibe transitions, ficus growing. Like a security camera timelapse but charming. This would be the single most shareable feature — people would screen-record and post it. Large effort but massive delight payoff.
